title: Genuine SMD RGB Cherry MX Switch 3-pin Mechanical Keyboard Speed Silver Mute red Blue Pink Switch (65pcs, Blue 3 pin)
description:
Size:104 pcs  |  Color:Speed Silver 3 pin
Details
This link is only the shaft and switches for mechanical keyboard,no keyboard and switch tester included.
Cherry switches
Cherry MX Black: Linear Switch,Actuation Force is 60cN
Cherry MX Red: Linear Switch,Actuation Force is 45cN
Cherry MX Pink: A quieter edition of cherry red,called Cherry pink as usual
Cherry MX Blue: Tactile and Clicky Switch,Actuation Force is 50cN
Cherry MX Brown: Tactile and non-clicky switch,Actuation Force is 45cN,it's soft tactile
Cherry MX Silver: Linear switches,40% faster than cherry red,little heavier than red,good for FPS player
Pls note: This switches can be used with PCB smd led,such as GK61 GK64.No switch led hole on the top cover.
2 pin 4 pin switch plug led can't be used directly with this switches.
features: This link is only the shaft and switches for mechanical keyboard,no keyboard and switch tester included.
Pls note: This switches can be used with PCB smd led,such as GK61 GK64 mechanical edition.
GK61 optical keyboard can't use it.Optical keyboard need optical switches
2 pin 4 pin switch led can't be used directly with this switches,such as 2x3x4 and 3mm and 1.8mm leds
SMD switches uses together with SMD RGB PCB,the effect is much better than normal switches
Note: During the delivery process, the pins of the switch may touch and bend. This is inevitable. The bent pin can be straightened with nail clippers, which will not affect normal use.

reviews.title: Great Authentic Cherry Switches
reviews.text: Great authentic Cherry switches. I'm writing this review on them now. They come in a zip loc bag and a few had bent pins. That is to be expected, I always order more than I actually need for this very reason.

reviews.title: Best keycaps I have ever used.
reviews.text:
Bought these for my GMMK pro.

I have owned several mechanical keyboards, but never bought a high-end set of keycaps. Unlike every other set I've ever used, these just seem to ignore dirt and grease. I can stuff my face with a big Mac and go straight to typing, and the caps stay completely clean. I don't know how that works but I won't question it.

Other keyboards require weekly wipe downs, I have never had to do it with these caps.

They're also noticeably built well. These are going to outlast my fingers by a hundred years, likely.

reviews.title: Legend doesn’t glow like other pics
reviews.text: For my Tofu65, I do like these pudding key caps, but I’m not sure why the legends on my key caps aren’t transparent. Everyone who posted reviews with pics show their legends glowing, so I’m not sure why mine are different. My other issue is I got several duplicative keys, and I’m missing a few. For example, I only have one home key and about 4 page down keys. So that’s slightly annoying. I’ve seen some others complain about this similar thing as well. But the feel and look of these key caps are quality. Slightly disappointed.

reviews.title: Great caps work as advertised
reviews.text: Works great

reviews.title: Frequent disconnections.
reviews.text: It works ok, but the mouse keeps disconnecting and they keyboard is sometimes unresponsive. Since I mostly game on my PC this is extremely annoying. I do like the look and feel of the keyboard and mouse, the disconnection issues are my only problem. (and since another wireless keyboard and mouse don't have the problem on the same PC, I know I don't have interference issues or badly set settings)

reviews.title: Works good
reviews.text: Bought this for my sons birthday. Gave it to him a little early. He seems to love it. He’s not had a complaint one about it.

reviews.title: Good quality!
reviews.text: The thickness is nice and has a good feel when typing. I personally like the font on these keys compared to a lot of the others found on Amazon. It's very clean and sleek. The colors are very pastel though, so in some lightings it's hard to differentiate between the two colors as there's not much contrast. I eventually just switched out the pastel purple caps for darker purple caps I already had. Overall they're really nice and fit my Anne Pro 2 perfectly. (Bonus: The case for storing the keys is also pretty fancy).

reviews.title: Poor man's white fox.
reviews.text:
I put them on a Quisan Magicforce 68 key mechanical keyboard. The legends and not as pronounced / readable as the stock key caps, they also seat closer to the board which leads to a shorter key press. Quite happy with the product for the price. They feel very nice.

Look grey in the photos but are bright white.

reviews.title: A great replacement for any stock ABS keycaps
reviews.text: A great replacement for any stock ABS keycaps! Not the highest quality PBT, but still thicker than ABS and its doubleshot with clean text. Some compatibility issues with space bar and shift keys(or any larger keys), due to these not having stabalizers. So they wiggle a little bit. But not uncomfortable or hard to use if you deal with it.

reviews.title: Amazing build quality!
reviews.text: These key caps are as advertised. Very high quality key caps with a variety of colors available. Was shipped in a very organize box with the keys all separated so you dont have to dig through the box to find any particular keys. So far I am satisfy with the product. Will see how it holds up

reviews.title: Spectacular!
reviews.text:
all i have to say is, get these keycaps. i was truly surprised at the quality of these keycaps. they feel really nice, solid, and with a little grain on the top that are very comfortable. Pricewise i think these are right on. At about $50 the quality is really good without the wait and the much higher prices of some other premium ABS...the colorway is one that i really like. I have always had a handful of colorways i wanted, the botonical, these Hammerhead, modern dolch, etc. the legends are pretty consistent with a few fonts thinner than others but i count 2 keys in my 60% board. not bad and i don't really notice it. i really like the color scheme more than i would notice the font issues.

Now i only wish i would have have a bigger board, 75% or TKL to take advantage of the other keys. geez keyboard world you will bankrupt me.

Very beautiful set. i'm coming back to my 60% for a little while.

reviews.title: Lovely colors, plenty of options!
reviews.text: Key caps look really great. Tons of combinations of caps for just about every layout. I threw it on an old WYSE WY-60 keyboard and it had some uncommon unit sizes for a few keys. Worked out great after finding keycaps that fit its spot. I had to grind down the edges of a few keys to fit them nicely. A modern keyboard will have no compatibility issues without a doubt!

I think it’s important to note these are not braided cables, and have a soft rubber feel, much like you would get on any standard USB type cable.

The USB-C plug is about 5.5mm thick, so should fit most recessed plugs. I was able to charge every device I tried, even a chromebook (lenovo duet) and phone (pixel 6a).

♫♫♫AJAZZ fruit series custom switches in order to satisfy you who like DIY, we introduce different tactile or linear switches, whether beginners, professional gamers, typists and programmers can choose the most suitable AJAZZ fruit series switches. Create a unique typing experience for you.

reviews.title: Light, snappy, like a punchier Gat brown
reviews.text:
I previously got a tactile switch sampler set and honestly loved the AJAZZ Banana more than U4 Boba and its other iterations. I liked these more than Glorious Panda and Holy Panda. I type a lot as a student, so I didn't want anything too heavy--it gets tiring. I've been using AJAZZ Banana for two days so far now and I LOVE it!

I was using lubed Gateron browns before. I wanted something punchier but not too heavy. Banana is the perfect answer to that. Actuation force is about the same to Gat browns. I have a north-facing RGB board and the lights come through Banana switches beautifully.

In answer to the other reviewer, I believe that the Banana switches ARE lubed (with what, I don't know). I have a stock/un-lubed AJAZZ Banana switch from my switch sampler pack, these pre-lubed ones feel slightly lighter in comparison. Out of the box, these feel great without additional lubing. Results will vary depending on your mech keeb setup.

Note that the switch pack comes with 46 switches, so it won't be enough to do a full board. I have a 60% board and had enough to do the alphanumeric keys and other frequently pressed keys like Shift, Enter, Ctrl, Backspace, Alt, Tab, and punctuation keys. I left my Gat browns on the less frequent keys. I have two "extra" Banana switches as backups.

Highly recommend. If you want tactile but not heavy, this is a very affordable option.

reviews.title: Bananas are excellent tactile switch alternatives to browns and blues.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:dff950435419a7006b0295e50b846c44]] This review is for the Ajazz Diced Fruit Banana Switch.
I wanted to try these switches compared to a brown switch because of its similar characteristics.
These Banana switches are definitely tactile and smooth. They do have a tactile click sound and require a bit more force to actuate (not really noticeable for me because I'm heavy handed and I bottom out the key when I type). I'm not going to mention specs such as actuation force and distance because you can find that info in the product description or manufacturer's site.
I will talk about the "feeling" of these switches though because in everyday use, gaming and office work, I'm not thinking about the actuation force/distance, etc. For me it is how it feels and sounds.

So first off, these are excellent switches, no doubt. It is then just a matter of personal preference whether or not you like the characteristics of the Banana switch. For me, it is a bit too noisy. The tactile bump feels good but after using the Banana's for a while, I would hate typing while on mic because the mic picks up the keystrokes loud and clear and can be annoying for others to hear. I don't have blue switches to compare these Bananas to but I would imagine the blue switches would be worse in sound. I do have red switches to compare and the reds are noticeably quieter, I think mainly due to being linear (no tactile bump). For me and personal preference and usage, I would stick with the reds.

If you like tactile switches and are currently using a different tactile switch, I'd urge you give these Banana switches a try. See my video review where I installed these switches into a keyboard and compare to red switches, so you can get an idea of the sound when the switches are installed into a keyboard frame with keycaps on. In the video I have mistakenly called the red switches Gaterons, but I'm pretty sure they are Kailh reds with box stem.

reviews.title: Nice looking strong tactile
reviews.text:
I love tactile switches, so when this was offered for review I figured I'd give it a shot as I'd heard good things about Ajazz switches. It arrived and I swapped out the switches in my NK65 (DROP Holy Pandas) and threw these into the alphas.

In terms of feel they're a pretty strong tactile. I felt it important to mention that I was using HP's above because these feel very similar to those, just with a lighter spring. The bump is close to identical which is nice, and I prefer the lighter spring as I've been mainlining my Niz lately with 35g switches, so light is my thing right now. In terms of sound, the housing does make these quite a bit higher pitch than HP's, and I'd honestly put it in line with some Akko tactiles. Not too high to not enjoy, but higher than I'm initially used to.

Because these come pre-lubed they can be used stock, which is nice because while once I start I find lubing switches to be relaxing, but it's an activity I dread until I'm actually going. Just something about it seems beyond tedious, and to be fair, it is. Thankfully the lube job on these is pretty good and there's no ping to be heard or scratchiness to be felt. I did lube one just to see the difference that one done by hand can make, and the job on these is good enough that it makes no difference. The switch is fine as-is.

Other than being plate mount only I can't find a problem with these. The spring weight is nice and they sound and feel great to type on. I'm thinking about grabbing another set to build out a board with them in the future.

reviews.title: Kiwi switches
reviews.text:
This is a review for the Kiwi Switch from DAIDAI AJAZZ. The Kiwi switch is tactile, 50gf, and housed in glittery translucent green. If you have clear keycaps, you can see the yellow stem and a little of the upper housing.

For the Kiwi switches, you get 46 switches. For most keyboards, 46 switches is not enough and you will need 2-3 cans.

The switches come in a clear plastic can, with a screw lid and a pull tab. The packaging is cute! More importantly, the can takes up less space than the usual plastic tray, and it’s reusable.

I use Akko Vintage Whites (linear), but I wanted to try tactile switches for the thumb clusters on my ergo keyboard, so I had feedback when I pressed those keys. The tactile bump is smooth and pronounced, I definitely know I pressed that key.

I didn’t notice any difference between these pre-lubed Kiwi switches and non-lubed switches.

reviews.title: Pretty good tactile switches
reviews.text: I've tried linears and even clicky switches, but I really prefer tacticles. These are right in the sweet spot for me in terms of spring weight and tactile bump. Nice colors, if that matters to you. I got these to try out before buying a full set, and I like them quite a bit.

reviews.text: These are way cooler than the standard cords! Just look at them guys.

reviews.title: Beautiful cable, working perfectly
reviews.text: I've wanted a coiled cable for a while but they're all so expensive. Found this epomaker one and I've bought products from epomaker before and haven't had any issues, and since this cable was only $20 I figured I could risk it. I was even happier when I saw they had a lavender option, most places just have light pink but not lavender. This cable matches my keycaps perfectly. The coiled part lays flat right out of the box (I've seen a few from other companies where the coiled part kinda curls up, which I didnt want), the cable feels nice and sturdy and good quality, and the metal connector also feels nice and heavy. I also like that this is a woven cable and not just plastic/rubber wrapped. Had no issues with the keyboard working after plugging in the cable. Absolutely worth the $20, I might buy the light pink one to match my other keyboards.

title: DROP + Matt3o MT3 Susuwatari Custom Keycap Set, ABS Hi-Profile Keycaps, Doubleshot Legends, Cherry MX Compatible Set for Ortholinear Mechanical Keyboards (Ortholinear)
features: A GROUNDBREAKING PROFILE, BACK FOR MORE: Sculpted in Hi-Profile, Matt3o’s groundbreaking MT3 /dev/tty custom keycap set changed the way we type.

FROM THE IBM TERMINAL TO THE DESKTOP: Based on the profile form the iconic IBM beamspring terminals of the 1970s, this custom set is fully sculpted in Hi-Profile. From its heavily slanted design to the scooped, contoured tops of each cap, the set recreates the feel of vintage keyboards.

DOUBLESHOT LEGENDS: As opposed to a dye-sublimiation process, these doubleshot legends are permanently molded inside the keycap, thus becoming an integral part of it. This ensures reliable legends and continuity throughout the set.
ORTHOLINEAR: For ortho keyboards like the Drop Planck, Preonic, and other boards with similar layouts.
SPECS: Produced by Drop and designed by Matt3o, ABS Hi-Profile MT3 keycaps, Doubleshot legends, MX compatible, and Fully sculpted based on IBM beamspring keycaps.

reviews.title: Absolute necessity on ortholinear boards
reviews.text:
These keycaps are thick, double shot so they will never fade, and sculpted. With ortholinear keyboards, these keycaps really help adjusting to smaller modifer key size and different key layout.

Pros:
1. Thick: This helps lower the pitch of the sound, which makes it sound quieter.
2. Doubleshot ABS: No wearing out the legends. The font is also fairly thick, making it much more legible than some other keycaps.
3. MT3 profile: The sculpted profile really makes it very comfortable to use and helps reduce hitting the wrong keys. No sharp edges due to the spherical shape. Very enjoyable for typing. The profile is much higher though so that could be an issue for some people.
4. Multiple profile options for certain keys: For example, the Ctrl and Esc keycs come in profiles that would allow it to be the top row or 3rd row. Space bar comes as two 1u, two 1.5u, and one 2u.
5. Storage tray and box: This seems silly to put as a pro but the tray and box it comes with make it easy to store unused or keycaps it replaced. Other Keycaps come in a zip lock bag that will just be a jumbled mess.

Cons:
1. Price. This is really expensive for a set of keycaps but this is the normal price for any specialty keycaps. It is what it is.
2. Limitation of scultped profile: Putting key profiles meant for row 1 in row 3 feels awkward so you might end you designing your keymap partially based on keycap profiles. Also might end up rotating some keycaps like row 5 Raise or Lower layer keycaps to make it more comfortable.
3. ABS option only. The PBT version is in white only. So if you prefer the color with the material that it isn't made it, then you're out of luck.
4. Limited to buying sets if you need extra keys: If you need more keys, you have to order a set. I would like to have more 1.5u blank keycaps but don't want to buy multiple sets just for the few pieces I want.

reviews.title: I like them
reviews.text:
These are my daily drivers, I use them on a Drop CTRL with Glorious Panda switches. Initially I thought the MT3 profile was disorienting, but after using them for a while, I am quite fond of it. I acknowledge that not everyone will like this profile though, and that should be considered if you are buying this product (or any like it). If you can find an MT3 set to try first before dumping about two hundred dollars on keycaps, it would be a **very good** idea. Other things go without saying, the thickness is very good the weight on my switches feels good and not like its making keys too easy to press or anything. I really like the theme, but all of that considered, I bought this specifically for the VI keys (hjkl with arrows) because I thought they looked super cool.

The price for these keycaps is a good deterrent for most people, for good reason. However this high of a price is pretty standard for high quality keycaps, as well as the Drop.com-exclusive MT3 profile.

This leads me to the value for money, its expensive and definitely should only be bought if you have a hankering to spend money on some expensive keycaps or you really really like having a specific row of keycaps.
Brightness is a 1 star because they're not shine-through keycaps, as they should be.
Gaming is a 4 star because, like, Sure? nothing about these keycaps keep you from gaming.

reviews.title: Best 3rd party caps I've ever owned.
reviews.text:
I really liked the esthetics of these key caps because I was looking for something which resembled "Old Nasa" feel, and these were the best I could find. The ergonomics were also great and everything has a very nice and snug fit, unlike the original keycaps that came with my CTRL keyboard.

I only wish that the "additional" buttons came with the normal key caps for the money I spent on the original "package". $30 for about 10 extra keys I can use on my ten keyless is VERY steep.

reviews.title: Best keycap set
reviews.text: I love this set. Worth every penny.

reviews.title: Absolutely gorgeous
reviews.text: Wonderful keycaps , goes great on my Nyquist Keyboard, looks professional yet custom, I highly recommend.

reviews.title: Not doublshot
reviews.text:
These two keys: < and > have already worn through. These are not double shot keycaps. They are a semi-translucent keycap with a finish over it that wears through. Doubleshot has an injection molded insert inside the keycap with the translucent area cut out of the keycap.
Should you not care about that, here is some more info. They look okay in the light, but in the dark there is a strong screen glare off of them that can be distracting.

I rate one star for lying about the nature of the product.

reviews.title: Arrived missing key caps but overall pretty good if you have the right keyboard
reviews.text:
Not a keyboard, just key caps to "bling out" your existing keyboard.

I purchased the following keyboard and these key caps fit perfectly. The keyboard (not this item, which is only key caps) comes with a red plastic tool that you can use to pop out the key caps:

Redragon K551 Mechanical Gaming Keyboard RGB LED Rainbow Backlit Wired Keyboard with Red Switches for Windows Gaming PC (104 Keys, Black)

Even the font on the key caps matches the keyboard I bought separately, and the backlighting shines through the font.

The only down side: the set of key caps is missing about 1/4 of the keys (see attached picture).

I would give it all five stars if it had arrived containing all the key caps advertised in the product listing. It was a surprise prank gift for a coworker, which is why I made do with what we got.

title: Akko Keycaps Top Printed Shine-Through with ASA Profile, White Keycaps PBT Double-Shot Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards
features: SHINE-THROUGH KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Shine-through keycap sets! Come with extremely durable and resistant double-shot Shine-through keycaps, the legend shine through keycaps brings unique and great RGB effects.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Not the brightest shine-through, but for the material and price it's worth it.
reviews.text:
Akko's ASA profile is a taller profile kind of like SA. Feels great to type on and is fine for gaming if you don't mind the extra travel time on each keystroke. The finish on these has a great texture and it's PBT so it shouldn't wear out quickly. "Shouldn't" being relative since I tend to shred keycaps as though my fingers were first dipped in acid before typing.

30.00 for keycaps of this quality and for the number of keys is great especially in a hobby where everything is over priced, over hyped, and overly bogged down in group buys and pre-orders that take literal years to fulfill.

reviews.title: Shine-Through keycaps, look amazing
reviews.text: These shine-through keycaps look awesome.  They are super easy to install, like most keycaps.  It is just a bit time consuming.  I love being able to change out keys and switches and seem to collect keycaps.  These are great and sound and feel amazing.

reviews.title: These keycaps feel and look awesome (for the price)
reviews.text:
I bought these keycaps for a cheap mechanical keyboard I had lying around. I had put some cheap keycaps of a similar price but a different brand on them earlier this year. Didn't like those keycaps at all.

These keycaps have a more pleasant texture, not smooth but not quite as grainy. I love love love the greyscale color scheme, and the blank keycaps are really aesthetically pleasing. Obviously, if you can't type without looking at your keyboard you should purchase the keycaps that come with all the lettering, it probably won't take away much from your experience.

One small note, I would suggest caution when using the included keycap puller. It scratched the side of my spacebar and left a little black line. Unfortunately, it is on the side of the keyboard that faces me, so now every time I sit down to type I will see the little black scratch. So be careful when pulling these off. Otherwise, I love these colors and will definitely consider buying other products from this brand based on this level of quality for price.

I forgot to mention. I loved the packaging on these keycaps!! The other brand I bought from had the keycaps all together in a ziplock bag. This brand had the keycaps arranged in order in sleeves and it was so much better for putting the keycaps on! ok that's all.

title: Hyekit Custom Gaming Keyboard - Alice Layout 66 Keys - Support QMK/Via Programmable Key - Gasket Structure - USB C Wired Mechanical Keyboard Kit - RGB Hot Swappable Switches & Keycaps - Acrylic Shell
description:
Note:
This link only sells a kit, not a finished keyboard, you need to assemble it yourself, the kit does not include switches, keycaps, data cables, or stabilizers.
About This Item
1.FancyAlice66 keyboard kit Alice is equipped with 66 keys, ergonomic, yes typing is more comfortable.  2. Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches.  3. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, kit supports QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions.  4. South-facing RGB Backlit has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight makes the keyboard look more attractive.  5.Gasket Mount, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, which makes the keyboard more comfortable and soft to type, and the typing sound is more pleasant.  6. Acrylic stacking shell, the texture is very good, enjoy the fun of custom assembling the shell.  7. Wired mode, support USB-C, typing response is faster, Type-c interface adapts to more data lines.  8. The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. First, ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly.  9.Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers.
Package includes:
Keyboard case*1  PCB*1  Quiet sandwich cotton*1  Switch pad*1  Assemble some parts
features: 【Package Included】Keyboard case*1, PCB*1, Quiet sandwich cotton*1, Switch pad*1, Assemble some parts. No USB cables, switches, stabilizer, and keycaps. (Note, The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. you ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly. Let's enjoy the process of assembling the keyboard and the great sense of achievement brought by the successful assembly.)

【Custom Gaming Keyboard】Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches. Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions. Wired mode, supports USB-C, typing response is faster.

【Alice Layout】The Alice layout considers the natural input angle of the wrist from the perspective of ergonomics. When using the conventional arrangement keyboard for continuous high-intensity input work, the unnatural bending of the wrist will easily lead to hand fatigue, and the Alice layout can solve this problem very well, it takes into account the integrity of the keyboard and the relatively relaxed input state of the wrist.

【South-facing RGB Backlit】Alice66 has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight, the RBG Backlight effect is very bright even in the daytime. The lower lamp position is designed so that the light will not be blocked by the switch. The fully transparent bottom cover allows the 16 bottom lights to display their backlight effects without obstruction, making the keyboard look more attractive.

【Acrylic Gasket Structure】Alice66 is designed with Gasket structure, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, the sound is pure, and the feel is soft. This wonderful feel can make the typing process very pleasant and full of fun.

reviews.title: Sounds great with no mods
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:58cc2520e806ff1428542a1df7456920]] Assembly was pretty easy for this DIY kit. It even sounds great without any modifications. I decided to put poron switch pads on the pcb since the foam it came with did not line up properly. The feet that comes with this kit is too aggressive for my liking. I decided to remove it and put clear rubber bumpons

Did not come with stabilizers. I was hoping it does at this price point. Speaking of which, this kit can be had for cheaper on other retailers. But ordering from Amazon gave me more confidence.

My copy came with cracked and scuffed case. I messaged the seller directly and they were able to help and send me a new case promptly. They also helped with VIA setup on this keyboard.

Pros:
- easy assembly
- VIA support PCB
- great sound with little to no modifications
- south facing, per key RGB
- under glow lighting
- A+ great customer support from seller

Cons:
- price seems higher compared to other websites
- inconsistent craftsmanship
- no stabilizers included
- front of the keyboard can be wobbly with the aggressive feet riser installed

(Keycaps: MT3 2048; Switches: WS Aurora Clears)

Really like and enjoy them. P.S. the yellow keycaps ARE NOT part of the set, they are from another keycap set. I thought it would compliment the purple nicely :)

reviews.title: Nice keycaps, but holy crap they're snug
reviews.text:
These keycaps are probably best suited for soldered switches. If you're on a hot swap board, get ready to separate every key from the cap as you'll be pulling the entire assembly out EVERY TIME.

Other than my tolerance/keycap ripping issues these are solid and have a good sound indicative of a good pbt material.

reviews.title: Spacebars do not fit well
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:14b0c07be1bf5b90e20f388d925ce8c8]] The keycap set is great in terms of design, modifier key size options, and key legend for quality for price. Fitting for all keys is great, excepts for the large spacebars.
The space bars (both 6.25u and 7u) in my set don’t work smoothly and somehow get the stabs stuck (two different kinds of stabs as well). I tried them on two keyboards (one with 7u and other with 6.25u) and only these spacebars showed problems and the other spacebars I have do not show this problem. I am not sure really whats causing the space bar issue, maybe those pbt space keycaps are slightly bent (as I have read about) but visually they look okay.

reviews.title: Good Quality for the $ - Fits Cherry MX Switches
reviews.text:
This fit my Ducky mechanical keyboard without issue. They seem to be well made and fit tightly in my cherry MX switches. A couple observations, not sure it’s a positive or a negative but a point of observation:

1) They do not come with media key replacements. The blue ones you see in my image are from my original key caps

2) The height of the keys are slightly shorter than my Ducky OEMs. While initially this was a possible concern for me, having typed with the new keys for a couple weeks, seems to not impact typing at all.

3) The blue caps that came with the replacements are not the ones you see in my photo (Enter key). They have a slightly yellow / blue hue to them vs. the Ducky’s I’m using. Wanted my enter key to match the rest of the blue media keys so I mixed and matched. Some may like the blue that comes with the set, I just preferred my original blue keys color.

For the price, they fit the bill, can’t complain. We’ll see long term how the printing of the keys holds up / how quickly they wear down.

Wish list:

- Caps lock key had the word ‘Caps’ on it
- Windows button keys had something besides “Super”.

Overall good purchase.

title: CUSTOMIZATIONS Coiled USB c Cable, Customized Gaming Mechanical Keyboard Cable, Handwork Braided + PET mesh, Metal Plug, with Detachable Metal Aviator Connector (Black White)

The weaving adopts woven mesh + PET mesh double-layer weaving, and the resin mesh is both beautiful and durable.

[Metal aviator connector]: The metal cannon connector allows you to quickly connect different cables to the keyboard or other accessories. The pure copper is chrome-plated, the copper needle and the copper core are plated with 24K gold wire, and the shell adopts electroplating technology to achieve a bright texture, highlighting high-end and texture.

[Coiled USB C Cable, USB 2.0 compatible] The cable adopts Type-C interface design, aluminum alloy CNC shell, sandblasted surface, corrosion and oxidation resistance, and recessed design can match most keyboards.

The color black of these pieces appear much more grey compared to he caps my keyboard came with. And each piece has a little dimple at the top where they were cast or molded. Not the end of the world, but other sets don't have that. Also found small imperfections in several pieces. Time will tell how they do over prolonged use. Pretty good for the price, solid 4 of 5.

title: Litorange Silicone 500PCS 40A Clear Universal O-Ring Switch Dampeners Keycap Sealing Gasket Washer (Better Than Rubber) for Cherry MX Switch Keyboard and Mechanical Keyboard Keys Dampers
description:
★Products include：
500PCS 8mm * 5mm * 1.5mm O-Ring 2PCS Keyboard Brushes 2PCS Keycap Puller   ★ Litorange Keyboard Kit is the essential accessory kit to improve key actuation speed, reduce bottoming out sound, reduce the shock on your fingers, and to maintain a clean keyboard. ★ 500 Silicone O-Ring Dampeners limit noise on keys and provide faster actuation with a 2mm thickness | 40A Shore (Soft) Transparent O-Rings allow LED Backlighting to shine brightly through keys. ★ Round Keycap Puller lets you remove mechanical keys with little effort and no restraint | Cleaning brush and cloth help maintain the cleanliness and pristine condition of your keyboard. ★ Plastic Box holds all mod kit components and your gaming mouse for easy and safe storage. ★ 40A (SOFT) O-RINGS: Softer O-rings provide a softer padded landing with every keystroke that reduces the impact on your fingers and eliminates the bottoming out "clack" sound of your keyboard.
★ Sound Dampeners & Maintenance Kit
Every mechanical keyboard needs a little tender love and care.  The Litorange Mod Kit includes every tool necessary to clean, improve, and silence your mechanical gaming keyboard.  Reduce the loud clacking sound for quieter typing late at night and maintain the look of your keyboard. The Mechanical Keyboard Mod Kit comes with 500 O-Ring silicone sound dampeners.  Transparent rings allow your keyboard's LED lighting to shine through without obstruction.  The 2mm thick O-Rings provide faster actuation time to help you beat the competition.
features: ★ Material: Silicone (Better than rubber). Quantity: 500 Pieces. Size: 8 mm/0.3 inch outer diameter, 5 mm/0.2 inch in inner diameter and 1.5 mm/ 0.06 inch in thickness.
★ Litorange Keyboard Kit is the essential accessory kit to improve key actuation speed, reduce bottoming out sound, reduce the shock on your fingers, and to maintain a clean keyboard.
★ Can be used across a wide temperature range from -65 degrees F (-54 degrees C) to 450 degrees F (232 degrees C).
★ 500 Silicone O-Ring Dampeners limit noise on keys and provide faster actuation with a 2mm thickness | 40A Shore (Soft) Transparent O-Rings allow LED Backlighting to shine brightly through keys.
★ 40A (SOFT) O-Ring: Softer O-rings provide a softer padded landing with every keystroke that reduces the impact on your fingers and eliminates the bottoming out "clack" sound of your keyboard.

reviews.title: Not for Certain Noise
reviews.text:
Unfortunately these o-rings didn't help with the noise from my mechanical keyboard and switches. The noise from my keyboard comes from the sound the springs make when the button pops back up, and these o-rings work to muffle sound when the keys are pressed down. For keyboards with raised keys that don't bottom out, like mine, and for switches that make more noise when they come back up instead of when they go down, the o-rings are not effective for reducing noise.

Otherwise, the product is good, and I'm keeping the o-rings for when I eventually change out the switches on my keyboard entirely.

reviews.title: awesome!
reviews.text: awesome set of o-rings for the price.

reviews.title: Really nice modification for my keyboard
reviews.text: These cut down the travel and damp the clicks in my moonlander, great value.

reviews.title: Good value rings kit
reviews.text:
I was setting up two keyboards with lighter switches, and think that o-rings are important when you drop the force. This kit was a good value, and I like the brushes to clean the keyboards.

I would recommend a decent set of tweezers to push the rings down onto the key posts. I had a plastic set that was perfect for it ( they came with a pair of Moondrop IEMs ).

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Very good O-rings

title: EPOMAKER Airy 143 Keys ESA Profile ANSI ISO Layout Clear Shine Through Keycaps Set for Mechanical Keyboard, Compatible with MX-Clone Switches Cherry/Gateron/Kailh/Akko/Ajazz Switch(Airy Black)
features: 【EPOMAKER Airy Transparent Keycaps】EPOMAKER Airy Keycaps set is designed to fit on a standard ANSI/ISO keyboard with MX style switches. Perhaps you want to give your favorite AZERTY keyboards a makeover to match the modern design trends? Whatever your taste, we’re positive that this is the right choice for you! Shine-through RGB backlight creates an vivid atmosphere which plays an important role in play-ability and mood

【ESA Profile, Compatible with MX Switches】From the community, for the community. EPOMAKER ESA Profile is the best practice of our pursue. ESA profile has a angled and spherical top similar to SA profile. The height is medium but not too tall. It provides unique experience unlike other profiles. This profile is considered as a solid option for both gaming and quick or regular typing

【143 Keys Set, ANSI/ISO Layout Compatible】Apart from the basic full keycaps, this set also contains extra replacement keycaps, including Mac modifiers and ISO special keys, such as big Enter key, 1.25U Shift...Either you are using ANSI standard layout or you are more familiar with ISO layout, we cover it all for you. Simply get one set and meet all needs

【High-Quality Polycabonate Material with UV Printing】Pursuing an ultimate transparency, this set adopts premium PC material to mould the keycaps and printing the characters through digital printing process. The rigorous production ensures the keycaps survive *wear-resistant and scratch-resistant tests. *Note: the test result comes from the laboratory data may differ from actual usage scenes. Due to the material properties, PC keycaps may still be vulnerable to scratches and hand sweat/oil/cream

【Increased Compatibility】EPOMAKER Airy Clear keycaps set contains special shift sizes and modifiers like the 1u, 1.75u and 2u SHIFT key, so it is with more possibilities to customize your EPOMAKER keyboard, such as 60%/65%/75%/TKL/96%/1800-compact/100% keyboard. It also has split spacebar keys that you might need. A keycap set that makes your DIY journey worry-free

reviews.title: Kawaii !!
reviews.text: Comes with all the keys caps you need, love how smooth it feels. You may need to dim your keyboard brightness, found the light through the clear key caps can hurt your eyes after a while.

reviews.title: Exceptionally perfect feel EXCEPT no way to find your position!
reviews.text:
I wanted to love these key caps. They look great! They feel superb and their smooth texture is exactly what I wanted.

However, two major flaws: I didn't even know this was a thing to consider, but they don't have bumps on the positioning keys "F" and "J". So if you type a lot and remove your hands from the keys, you need to look down every time to find your position. This interrupts my workflow as a transcriptionist quite a bit. The other disadvantage is that the smooth feel of the material also makes it impossible to keep skin oil, snack residues, etc. from being left behind on these caps, making them somewhat difficult to grip after the first half hour.

reviews.title: Really nice visual upgrade
reviews.text:
I wanted to upgrade my keyboard’s appearance, and after much Googling I settled on this product. I have a full size 104 key keyboard, and looking at keycaps on various websites makes it seem kinda complicated to know which set I’d need for my specific keyboard (Das Keyboard 4). Some Ctrl keys are wider than others, Spacebars shorter than others, etc. and what I liked about this set is that it includes different length keys and you just use the one that fits your keyboard and ignore the rest.

This is my first set of keycaps so I can’t really speak with any knowledge to the quality of the keys, but I’m really happy with how they look. They feel similar to the ones that came with my keyboard but look muuuuch better.

reviews.title: Fantastic keycap set
reviews.text:
They finally arrived! I plan on putting these keycaps on a 75% keyboard that won't arrive for another couple of months so in the pictures, I only put it on part of my keyboard to test them out. I want to "preserve" that new keycap feeling later. :)

These keycaps are fairly thick and have the standard PBT texture. The doubleshot legends are quite clear and none of the spacebars are warped! This is a new profile I've never typed on and I can't find much information on the web about it so I included a lot of pictures. I compared them in the pictures to my Ducky keyboard with doubleshot ABS. They actually aren't too different from OEM keycap profile (from the Ducky) height but some of the rows are higher. They're much more shapely and the F and J keys have a deeper dish. Compared to DSA keycaps, they don't seem to be quite as deep dishey overall which I actually prefer.
The keycaps fit snug on cherry mx brown stems, more so than my Ducky keycaps. They aren't hard to put on though. Some of the stems have a small part of plastic missing from the MX cross area but it's only a handful of the keycaps and it didn't effect the fit. I've included photos of this as well.
Overall they're great keycaps that I won't be using yet until my keyboard for them arrives.
